summary: Checked the iteration-2 Define-phase artifacts (requirements.md R1-R33, open_questions.md, discovery_notes.md) against guides/backend-guide.md and guides/ui-style-guide.md. The work under review is deliberately non-technical business requirements plus discovery notes; there is no code, design, or diff, so the code-structural rules (services with .call -> Result, thin controllers, no business logic in callbacks/jobs, Tailwind type/spacing scale, semantic status colors, smallest-DOM-unit Turbo targeting, Minitest) have nothing to bind against yet and correctly defer to design/build, where this critic re-engages. Where the specs touch guide-governed surfaces they align rather than deviate: R4 and R25 require the paused and failure states be shown 'using a label and not color alone,' honoring the UI guide accessibility baseline (status never conveyed by color alone); R30 mandates plain, non-technical language, consistent with the Define-stays-non-technical constraint. The discovery notes accurately restate the mandatory guide constraints and map every proposed capability onto existing service POROs (Phases::SendBack target_step_id, AnswerQuestions, Approve, StepRuns::Queue) rather than callbacks, fat controllers, or model business logic. No requirement prescribes an approach that would deviate from either guide. One non-blocking observation: R15's 'restart in progress' state, unlike R4/R25, does not restate the label-not-color-alone rule, but the principle is already established doc-wide and carries forward to design. No blockers or deviations found.
